title: EoE what version to get
wikitext:
[]: EoE - what version to get?             Quote Post by Jay911 » Fri Mar 09, 2007 3:13 am Hi all, Sorry if this is a repost ... couldn't find a similar topic after searching. I am looking to replace my End of Evangelion DVD as, when I took it out to view it a few weeks ago, I noticed that the center of the disc is starting to delaminate (the part in between the media layer and where the hole is). I'd rather not have it break or worse, screw up a drive/DVD player. I can't reasonably tell from the versions available for sale on Amazon, etc., which one I should get. I'd rather get the one that has the better image quality (as I've been led to believe that there are varying levels of clarity in the various releases). The version I have now is the Manga release with a 2002 copyright date on the DVD case. It's got the cover art of Shinji & Asuka looking out over the sea of LCL and GNR's head. I see Amazon has two 2005 versions, which I figure one of the ones is what you folk are talking about. One has this same cover as my current copy, and the other, subtitled "Death & Rebirth: The End of Evangelion", has Kaworu standing before Lilith. I already have the series via the Platinum thinpak set. Is the one titled "Death & Rebirth: End of Evangelion" the EoTV version? Which should I get if I want EoE, the movie? Thanks in advance :)

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Fri Mar 09, 2007 4:50 am This is the version I have but that's not much good to you if you're in America. Death is a theatrical recap of the series and rebirth is most of ep 25' Air. Death has a few scenes that differ from the on Air version, but nothing really worth going out of your way to buy it. If you watch the dub I always thought that 'Death' had the best English version of Kaworu. All of the English language versions are of the same image quality unfortunatly.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Mar 09, 2007 6:56 am If you have the capability to play imported DVD's, then get the R2 Renewal Japanese DVD. They have superior video and audio. However, no subtitles. Otherwise, all the domestic releases are the same.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Mar 09, 2007 7:38 am Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: All of the English language versions are of the same suck-ass image quality unfortunately.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 09, 2007 8:18 am And they all have shitty subtitles, too -- so why pay for any? Buy your R2 Renewal disc today!

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Fri Mar 09, 2007 10:39 am Here's your best shot for the region 2, unsubtitled one; http://www.play-asia.com/paOS-13-71-a7-77-y-49-en-15-evangelion-70-7xl.html But Manga have promised to release it worldwide later this very year.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Fri Mar 09, 2007 1:25 pm Kaysow wrote: Here's your best shot for the region 2, unsubtitled one; http://www.play-asia.com/paOS-13-71-a7-77-y-49-en-15-evangelion-70-7xl.html But Manga have promised to release it worldwide later this very year.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Mar 09, 2007 1:27 pm Even if they do re-release it, they'll compromise precious space on the DVD, which could be used for video quality, for their shit-tastic dub.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Fri Mar 09, 2007 1:36 pm Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: When did they say this?
